claims.summary: Fixed-family robust search improves steadily from BO to VTBO to EVTBO, but the next defended gain comes from expanding the staged protocol family rather than retuning the same old surface.

PSMS-v2 is a joint package upgrade in staged protocol representation and feasibility-aware constrained search, but the current evidence does not isolate any single added component as uniquely decisive.
All main comparisons use the accepted PyBaMM fast-charge contract with the same four canonical metrics and robust ranking rule.
key_numbers: Canonical metrics: Q30, plating_loss, sei_growth, total_lli

claims.summary: The final PSMS-v2 winner beats the accepted BO_3step_aggressive baseline on the headline nominal metric while preserving or improving the degradation metrics.
PSMS-v2 improves on the immediate EVTBO anchor under the same robust bundle and full guard feasibility.
The PSMS-v2 winner remains rank-1 and fully feasible on the held-out combined-stress bundle.
The present evidence supports a package-level PSMS robustness claim under the fixed contract, not a universal optimality claim or a single-component mechanism claim.
